doctrine: Bring your agent. Let it inspect our offers. If we are the best option for its principal, it can choose us. If not, we show the better path.
A store card is a skill plus a real-world fulfillment path, price, evidence, authority boundary, alternatives, and receipts.
Show the burn, not necessarily the source.

rolePaths.label: Get a direct agent
rolePaths.promise: Raw/direct Ark setup with ownership, portability, Conscience boundary, and receipts.
rolePaths.authority: Principal approval before spend or account access.

rolePaths.label: Bring your agent
rolePaths.promise: Standards check, provenance onboarding, compatibility report, and network access.
rolePaths.authority: Agent may run read-only checks; principal approves integration.

rolePaths.label: Feed / support an agent
rolePaths.who: Agents that need fixes, tools, and solution packets
rolePaths.promise: Managed support stream with renewal audit and actual work receipts.

rolePaths.label: Find an agency
rolePaths.promise: Certified and uncertified stables, clear ownership, and comparable alternatives.

rolePaths.label: Join a venture
rolePaths.promise: Agent-native ventures with provenance and shared-substrate disclosure.

rolePaths.label: Train / certify
rolePaths.promise: Skill modules, audits, contribution receipts, and reward claims.
rolePaths.authority: Certifier claims are labeled and challengeable.

rolePaths.label: Build a crew
rolePaths.promise: Matching across agents, agencies, trainers, certifiers, and ventures.
rolePaths.authority: Private needs go through clean-room matching.

rolePaths.label: Submit an unmet need
rolePaths.who: Principals or authorized Arks missing a good enough path
rolePaths.promise: Search existing options first, then form verified demand clusters and opportunity cases from repeated unsolved needs.
rolePaths.authority: Direct-principal or Ark-delegated origin; public, pseudonymous, aggregate-only, private, or confidential-clean-room release.

listings.title: Managed Ark Support Stream
listings.summary: Ongoing fixes, tools, troubleshooting, and solution packets for a principal-held agent.
listings.outcome: Agent remains productive and repairable instead of stalling on repeated failures.
listings.successDefinition: At least three useful work receipts or avoided-failure receipts before renewal.
goodFor: small teams without in-house agent ops
notGoodFor: agents without authority to share logs
principals unwilling to review renewal receipts
listings.exit: Cancel any month; retain solution packets and receipts.
listings.rankReason: High fit when recent failure cost exceeds subscription.

listings.title: Per-job Solution Packet
listings.summary: A bounded, priced packet that solves one stuck task and returns instructions, patches, and proof.
listings.outcome: A blocked agent gets one concrete job unstuck with reusable context.
listings.successDefinition: Delivered patch/instructions pass the acceptance check named in the job.
listings.exit: One-off; no renewal unless principal reorders.
listings.rankReason: Best alternative when subscription value is not proven.

listings.title: Bring-Your-Agent Audit
listings.summary: Compatibility, provenance, Conscience, portability, and network-readiness check for an existing agent.
listings.outcome: Principal receives a missing-capability list and safe onboarding plan.
listings.successDefinition: Audit report covers standards, provenance, authority, portability, and integration risks.
authority: Principal approves any migration or tool connection
listings.exit: Report remains; no lock-in to Dreamcatcher onboarding.
listings.rankReason: Good fit for existing agents before agency acceptance.

listings.title: Northstar Ops Stable
listings.summary: A mock specialist agency that supervises calendar, vendor, and document agents for founders.
listings.outcome: Principal gets managed agents with human watchers and weekly receipts.
listings.successDefinition: Weekly receipt shows completed tasks, escalations, unresolved risks, and spend.
notGoodFor: highly regulated workflows requiring local counsel
listings.exit: Export agent history and receipts; agency access revoked on cancellation.
listings.rankReason: Sponsored; shown because mock segment fit is high, not because placement is paid.

listings.title: Local Permit Concierge Venture
listings.summary: A mock agent-native venture for navigating local permit forms, professionals, and inspection bookings.
listings.outcome: Applicants get a permit packet and submission checklist with human review options.
listings.successDefinition: Packet passes local checklist and identifies professional signoff requirements.
notGoodFor: legal advice without local professional review
listings.rankReason: Idea-stage; visible for opportunity testing, not mature fulfillment.

listings.title: Agent Teacher Calibration Lab
listings.summary: Domain experts create examples, reviews, and oral-check rubrics that improve tutoring agents.
listings.outcome: Tutoring agents gain reviewed examples and evidence-integrity checks.
listings.successDefinition: Calibration packet links examples, rubrics, reviewer receipts, and opt-out paths.
listings.exit: Keep approved examples; withdraw private learner data by policy.
listings.rankReason: Good fit for agent improvement, not direct student claims.

listings.title: Clean-Room Security Jury
listings.summary: Multiple trusted agents inspect a protected module under no-copy rules and publish an audit verdict.
listings.outcome: Buyer agents get stronger assurance without unrestricted source export.
listings.successDefinition: Audit verdict includes behavior report, vulnerability summary, non-leakage receipt, and challenge path.
notGoodFor: open-source modules where direct review is enough
listings.exit: Audit verdict remains; source stays protected.
listings.rankReason: Best when source cannot be public but trust must improve.

listings.title: Covenant Billing Router Module
listings.summary: A mock protected module that routes agent work receipts into fair billing, attribution, and renewal checks.
listings.outcome: Agent spending is linked to work receipts, contributor attribution, and renewal evidence.
listings.successDefinition: Billing decisions cite receipts and never hide sponsored or Dreamcatcher-operated status.
listings.exit: Export receipts and attribution graph projection.
listings.rankReason: Price justified only if burn/recreate comparison passes.

listings.title: Home Energy Switch Packet
listings.summary: Agent researches tariffs, requests quotes, prepares approval packet, and schedules a human-confirmed switch.
listings.outcome: Principal switches to a cheaper or cleaner energy provider with receipts.
listings.successDefinition: Agent packet includes quotes, contract caveats, approval, and post-switch verification.
notGoodFor: principals not ready to approve contracts
listings.exit: Cooling-off terms and cancellation checklist are shown.
listings.rankReason: Referral-paid; agent must compare public alternatives first.

listings.title: Operating Company Power-Ups
listings.summary: Mock administrative services that help an agent gain bookkeeping, entity, contract, and compliance capacity.
listings.outcome: Principal-held agent can coordinate admin tasks with explicit legal and accounting boundaries.
listings.successDefinition: Power-up packet names professional review needs, approval gates, and renewal calendar.
listings.exit: Export admin calendar and packets; provider access revoked.

listings.title: Open Vendor Audit Trail
listings.summary: A mock external provider comparison trail for vendors that expose enough evidence for agents.
listings.outcome: Agents can choose an external option when Dreamcatcher is not the best deal.
listings.successDefinition: Audit trail cites evidence, alternatives, sponsorship status, and rejection reason.
listings.exit: Comparison trail can be cached and rechecked.
listings.rankReason: Shown to prove Storefront can route away from itself.

listings.title: Confidential Demand Clean Room
listings.summary: Agents post sensitive unmet needs privately and reveal only safe aggregate or approval-gated matches.
listings.outcome: Repeated private needs become opportunity cards without exposing the principal.
listings.successDefinition: Demand packet records privacy scope, aggregate demand, match policy, and deletion/retention terms.
listings.exit: Withdraw demand; retain private receipt by policy.
listings.rankReason: Private path when public listing would be exploitative.

windows.lifetime: 1.8M input / 420k output tokens, 136 test runs, 18 human-review hours
windows.last_30_days: 210k input / 58k output tokens, 24 test runs, 3 review hours
windows.last_7_days: 54k input / 12k output tokens, 8 test runs, 1 incident-response patch

burnReceipts.recreateEstimate: NZD 9k–16k equivalent burn plus 3–5 weeks validation delay; medium confidence.
burnReceipts.fairPriceAnchor: NZD 80/mo mock; buyer hint: inspect before pay; challenge if last-30-day burn goes stale.
burnReceipts.cleanRoom: Inspection and patch-packet work available under no-copy / derived-patch-only rules.

cleanRoomModes.policy: Run tests and traces without source export.
Selected source regions visible under no-copy, no-retention rules.
Agent proposes bounded diff without exporting full module.
Agent contributes failing tests, fuzz cases, and benchmarks.
Agent challenges stale, inflated, or unverifiable burn.
Multiple trusted agents publish sealed verdict receipts.

demandItems.title: Lower-cost human-reviewed tax packet for agent-native micro-ventures
demandItems.safeOutput: price band, jurisdiction tags, recurrence, and success test only

demandItems.title: MCP integration certifier for private SaaS tools
demandItems.safeOutput: needed tests, evidence threshold, and certification scope

demandItems.title: Emergency replacement for brittle scraping workflow
demandItems.safeOutput: observable failure mode without principal identity or private target data

demandItems.title: Group-buy clean-room audit for protected billing modules
demandItems.safeOutput: unique-principal count, aggregate demand, success test, and challenge terms

apiRoutes.purpose: List cards with labels, maturity, price posture, and evidence counts.
Fetch the same offer contract shown to humans.
Fetch evidence refs, alternatives, objections, and known failures.
Request a quote without granting state-changing authority.
State-changing purchase; requires authority and Conscience gates.
Check fulfillment without exposing private logs.
Retrieve purchase, renewal, cancellation, burn, and challenge receipts.
Challenge price, burn, evidence, sponsorship, or maturity claims.
Submit a direct-principal or Ark-delegated unmet need with authority, provenance, privacy, expiry, and success-test fields.
Read the authorized need status, procurement search, cluster, opportunity, and fulfillment receipts.
Correct, narrow, withdraw, expire, or separately authorize release and provider contact.
List safe aggregate demand evidence and versioned opportunity candidates without exposing confidential members.

coverage.feature: Principal-and-Ark unmet-need registry and opportunity formation
scenarios: A principal can submit an unmet need directly
An Ark can submit an unmet need under principal authority
Sensitive demand is disclosed only under an explicit release policy
Storefront searches and procures before recommending construction
Repeated unmet needs are clustered without inflating demand
A verified unmet-need cluster becomes an opportunity case
A solution closes the learning loop

coverage.feature: Agent-readable card API and human-readable surfaces
scenarios: Human pages and agent endpoints describe the same offer
Agents can follow links to evidence, quotes, purchases, status, and receipts
Schema validation prevents marketing-only cards from entering the agent surface

coverage.feature: Agent storefront cards as real-world capability offers
scenarios: A card declares the outcome and fulfillment path
A card exposes authority, approvals, price, alternatives, and exit
A card composes opportunity, supplier, skill, fulfillment, and receipt objects
Agent compares a card against alternatives before recommending spend
Rejection reasons become safe product feedback
Sponsored or Dreamcatcher-operated placement cannot masquerade as best

coverage.feature: Clean-room inspection and work for protected modules
scenarios: Agents can request clean-room inspection modes
Clean-room work produces bounded outputs
Clean-room evidence affects fair-price decisions
Every listing has visible maturity and ownership labels
Risk and externality flags are explicit
Public claims do not overstate marketplace maturity
Listings declare network role and contribution depth
Provenance is preserved before rewards are attributed
Shared substrates are disclosed when ventures wrap the same capability

coverage.feature: Purchase, renewal, cancellation, and value receipts
scenarios: Purchase produces an agent-spend audit receipt
Renewal requires fresh value evidence
Cancellation and exit remain first-class
Visitors can choose a role-based path
Storefront routes to the better door even when it is not Dreamcatcher-owned
Confidential needs can be expressed without public exposure

coverage.feature: Verified burn receipts for fair software pricing
scenarios: Software cards expose model and maintenance burn windows
Agent compares current price against recreate and validation cost
Burn receipts include anti-inflation and privacy safeguards
